title: DP World subsidiary wins top Dubai Neurotechnology Innovation Award in 2026 | HealthCare Middle East & Africa - No.1 Health & Pharma Manufacturing Industry Publication
summary: The award places the Centre among the leading innovators in healthcare, particularly in neurotechnology and personalised rehabilitation.
thbAction: Pitch THB's diagnostics CRM to help labs manage doctor outreach, collection-center expansion, and B2B referral relationships while improving conversion and retention.
contentHtml:
UAE—The Brain & Performance Centre, a DP World company based in the UAE, has been named Best Integrated Neurotechnology Innovation – Dubai 2026 at the FDI Insider Awards.

The recognition highlights the Centre’s growing role in combining advanced clinical science with technology-driven brain health solutions and performance optimisation.

The award places the Centre among the leading innovators in healthcare, particularly in neurotechnology and personalised rehabilitation.

It also reflects the increasing global interest in integrated approaches that connect diagnostics, treatment, and long-term cognitive and physical wellbeing.

Integrated model for Brain Health and Performance

The Brain & Performance Centre has built its model around combining clinical expertise with advanced diagnostic and performance tools.

The Centre integrates neuroimaging, cognitive assessment, and physical evaluation into a unified care pathway for individuals with neurological conditions and those seeking performance enhancement.

Depending on each programme, clients may undergo a range of assessments, including MRI scans, SPECT imaging, neurocognitive testing, and physical performance analysis.

These tools help clinicians build a detailed understanding of brain function and overall health status, which supports more targeted and personalised care planning.

A multidisciplinary team then reviews the collected data and designs tailored programmes that address both recovery and optimisation goals.

This approach allows the Centre to align medical insight with measurable performance outcomes.

Technology-driven personalised care

The Centre applies technology at every stage of the patient journey, from initial assessment to continuous monitoring.

It uses cognitive training systems, wearable devices, and physical performance tracking tools to observe progress in real time.

These data points, combined with clinical observations, allow specialists to adjust programmes as needed.

 As a result, care remains dynamic, responsive, and closely aligned with individual needs.

The model also strengthens the Centre’s focus on long-term neurological health and rehabilitation outcomes.

The integration of multiple data streams supports more informed decision-making and reinforces the Centre’s commitment to evidence-based, personalised care delivery.

Leadership perspective and vision

Dr. Craig Cook, CEO of The Brain & Performance Centre, said the recognition reflects the organisation’s commitment to advancing brain health through innovation and collaboration.

He noted that the Centre continues to focus on delivering meaningful and measurable outcomes for individuals and families through a combination of clinical expertise and technology.

He also emphasized the importance of personalised care in addressing complex neurological and cognitive needs, particularly as demand for advanced brain health services continues to grow globally.

UAE’s growing role in healthcare innovation

The award comes as the UAE strengthens its position as a hub for advanced healthcare innovation and medical technology.

Institutions across the country continue to invest in precision medicine, digital health systems, and rehabilitation technologies that support long-term wellness and performance.

Within this landscape, the Brain & Performance Centre continues to expand its role in neurotechnology-driven care, focusing on cognitive health, neurological recovery, and performance enhancement supported by data-led insights and multidisciplinary expertise.
